system_context: Node.js REST API running in production on AWS Lambda. Stack: TypeScript, Express, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL RDS. Error rate on POST /users spiked to 34% overnight. CloudWatch logs show 500 errors but stack traces are not captured. The endpoint creates new user accounts. Local development works fine.
turns: Started investigating the POST /users 500 errors. Read the CloudWatch logs - errors started at 02:14 AM UTC. The log message is just 'Internal server error' with no stack trace. This means the error is being swallowed somewhere in the error handler.

Read src/api/users.ts. The POST handler calls userService.createUser(body). Read src/services/user.service.ts. The createUser method calls prisma.user.create(). There is a try/catch but it only logs to console.error and re-throws. The error handler in src/middleware/error.ts catches it and returns 500 without the stack trace in production.

Checked src/middleware/error.ts. In production mode, it returns { error: 'Internal server error' } without any detail. This is correct for security but makes debugging impossible. Need to add error tracking. But first need to find the actual error.

Added temporary detailed logging to src/api/users.ts to capture the full error object and send it to CloudWatch with console.error(JSON.stringify(error)). Deployed to staging which has similar data to production.

Reproduced the error in staging. The full error is: PrismaClientKnownRequestError: Unique constraint failed on the fields: ('email'). Code: P2002. This is a unique constraint violation on the email field. But why is it happening at 34% rate? Users shouldn't be sending duplicate emails that frequently.

Investigated the frontend code in the mobile app. Found a bug: the submit button does not disable after first click. Users on slow connections are double-tapping the submit button, sending the same POST /users request twice within milliseconds. The first request succeeds and creates the user. The second request hits the unique constraint.

Read the database schema in prisma/schema.prisma. The email field has @unique. The Prisma error code P2002 is correct. The fix needs to happen at the API level - we need to handle the unique constraint error gracefully and return a 409 Conflict instead of 500.

Updated src/services/user.service.ts to catch Prisma errors and rethrow them as typed application errors. Added PrismaClientKnownRequestError check for code P2002 to throw a ConflictError with message 'Email already exists'.

Updated src/middleware/error.ts to handle ConflictError and return 409 status with { error: 'Email already exists' } response. Updated the error hierarchy in src/errors/index.ts to add ConflictError extending AppError with statusCode 409.

Added idempotency at the API level: if the same email is submitted within 5 seconds, return the existing user record instead of erroring. Implemented using a simple in-memory cache with TTL. This handles the double-tap case cleanly.

Wrote tests for the new error handling in src/services/__tests__/user.service.test.ts. Added 4 tests: duplicate email returns 409, unique email succeeds, idempotency returns same user on duplicate within TTL, idempotency creates new user after TTL expires.

All tests passing. Deployed fix to staging. Tested double-tap scenario: first request creates user, second request within 5 seconds returns same user with 200. Error rate in staging: 0%. Ready to deploy to production.

Deployed to production. Monitoring CloudWatch. Error rate on POST /users dropped from 34% to 0.1% (the remaining 0.1% are actual duplicate email attempts from different sessions, correctly returning 409). Fix confirmed successful.
